Comment: Not clear on any of the material. His tests do not reflect what he goes over in class. He purposely tries to trick you whenever he can. Doesn't feel any remorse in failing a student no matter how hard they try. DO NOT TAKE AND WAIT FOR MURTHI TO TEACH IT.

Being a graduate level class, the difficulty level was normal. No midterms no final, so definitely have to start early on the assignments and projects. To get a good grade needs a high level of effort and interest though.  He wants the students to understand the material in the class which may be hard for some (depends on your background).
Terrible Professor. It is worth it to wait a semester and take it with Murthi. He is the only engineering teacher I have had that does not care one bit if he fails half the class. Which he did.

Class is very difficult, but not impossible. He grades based on effort and understanding. If you put in the time for homework, you'll do fine on the tests. Start early, because he is rarely in his office for support, as he travels frequently. Overall a great professor, but he expects a lot from the students.

Loads up powerpoints with general equations, sometimes so many that you cant tell what goes with what. Does not do any concrete examples, does not work things out in class. Expects you to be able to do everything on your own. Get it the first time he goes over it or figure it out on your own. Don't bother asking to go through "simple algebra"

His exams weren't tough but he didn't prepare us well enough to do good on them. He did not teach the material well. He expects you to just know stuff. The highest score out of 100 was like a 25. he is very hard on partial credit.  He did curve at the end, but not knowing EEN336 will only haunt u for EEN436. my advice: take Murthi instead.

Bad news. He did not follow his own syllabus. we were supposed to have three exams during the semester.
